nodes.description: Teeth that are present are characteristically conical or peg-shaped, a distinctive dental feature of HED.

evidence.reference_title: Hypohidrotic Ectodermal Dysplasia.
evidence.snippet: bonding of conical teeth

nodes.description: EDARADD is the intracellular adaptor protein that bridges the EDAR receptor to downstream NF-κB signaling. Upon EDA ligand binding to EDAR, the receptor's intracellular death domain recruits EDARADD, which in turn recruits TRAF6 to activate the IKK complex and NF-κB transcription factors. Loss of EDARADD function completely blocks this signaling cascade, preventing NF-κB-dependent transcriptional programs required for ectodermal appendage initiation and morphogenesis.

evidence.reference_title: Gene defect in ectodermal dysplasia implicates a death domain adapter in development.
evidence.snippet: This adapter, which we have called Edaradd (for Edar-associated death domain), interacts with the death domain of Edar and links the receptor to downstream signalling pathways.

evidence.reference_title: Ectodysplasin A/Ectodysplasin A Receptor System and Their Roles in Multiple Diseases.
evidence.snippet: The former binds to the EDA receptor (EDAR), resulting in the recruitment of the intracellular EDAR-associated death domain (EDARADD) adapter protein and the activation of the NF-κB signaling pathway

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in EDARADD account for a small proportion of hypohidrotic ectodermal dysplasia cases.
evidence.snippet: EDARADD interacts with the TAB2/TRAF6/TAK1 complex, which is necessary for NF-kappaB activation by EDAR.

evidence.reference_title: Characterization of EDARADD gene mutations responsible for hypohidrotic ectodermal dysplasia.
evidence.snippet: Nuclear factor (NF)-κB reporter assays demonstrated that all the mutant EDARADD showed reduction in activation of NF-κB.

nodes.description: Dry, thin skin due to reduced eccrine gland function and ectodermal abnormalities.

evidence.reference_title: Hypohidrotic Ectodermal Dysplasia.
evidence.snippet: Skin care products for eczema and exposures that exacerbate dry skin.

evidence.reference_title: Novel homozygous frameshift insertion variant in the last exon of the EDARADD causing hypohidrotic ectodermal dysplasia in two siblings: case report and review of the literature.
evidence.snippet: very dry skin

evidence.reference_title: Gene defect in ectodermal dysplasia implicates a death domain adapter in development.
evidence.snippet: We also identify a missense mutation in its human orthologue, EDARADD, that is present in a family affected with hypohidrotic ectodermal dysplasia.

evidence.reference_title: Autosomal dominant anhidrotic ectodermal dysplasias at the EDARADD locus.
evidence.snippet: We demonstrated that the p.Leu112Arg mutation completely abrogated NF-kB activation, whereas the p.Glu142Lys retained the ability to significantly activate the NF-kB pathway.

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in EDARADD account for a small proportion of hypohidrotic ectodermal dysplasia cases.
evidence.snippet: We identified one EDARADD 6-bp homozygous in-frame deletion (c.402-407del, p.Thr135-Val136del) in a patient born to consanguineous parents.

evidence.reference_title: Only four genes (EDA1, EDAR, EDARADD, and WNT10A) account for 90% of hypohidrotic/anhidrotic ectodermal dysplasia cases.
evidence.snippet: These four genes accounted for 92% (56/61 patients) of HED/EDA cases

evidence.reference_title: Novel homozygous frameshift insertion variant in the last exon of the EDARADD causing hypohidrotic ectodermal dysplasia in two siblings: case report and review of the literature.
evidence.snippet: targeted next-generation sequencing analysis yielded the novel homozygous insertion variant c.322_323insCGGGC p.(Arg108ProfsTer7) in EDARADD.

nodes.description: Inability to tolerate warm environments due to impaired thermoregulation from defective sweating. Episodes of hyperthermia are a major clinical concern, especially in early childhood.

evidence.reference_title: Hypohidrotic Ectodermal Dysplasia.
evidence.snippet: leading to episodes of hyperthermia until the affected individual or family acquires experience with environmental modifications to control temperature.

nodes.description: Reduced number of teeth (oligodontia or anodontia) with characteristically conical or peg-shaped teeth when present.

evidence.reference_title: Hypohidrotic Ectodermal Dysplasia.
evidence.snippet: Only a few abnormally formed teeth erupt, at a later-than-average age.

evidence.reference_title: Autosomal dominant anhidrotic ectodermal dysplasias at the EDARADD locus.
evidence.snippet: Anhidrotic ectodermal dysplasia (EDA) is a disorder of ectodermal differentiation characterized by sparse hair, abnormal or missing teeth, and inability to sweat.

nodes.description: Reduced or absent sweating due to defective eccrine sweat gland development. Leads to heat intolerance and risk of hyperthermia. Sweating, although present in some patients, is greatly deficient.

evidence.reference_title: Hypohidrotic Ectodermal Dysplasia.
evidence.snippet: Sweating, although present, is greatly deficient, leading to episodes of hyperthermia until the affected individual or family acquires experience with environmental modifications to control temperature.

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in EDARADD account for a small proportion of hypohidrotic ectodermal dysplasia cases.
evidence.snippet: Hypohidrotic ectodermal dysplasia (HED) is characterized by abnormal development of the eccrine sweat glands, hair and teeth.

nodes.description: Disrupted EDA-EDAR-EDARADD signaling impairs hair follicle induction and cycling, resulting in hypotrichosis with sparse, fine, and slow-growing scalp hair and reduced body hair.

evidence.reference_title: Hypohidrotic Ectodermal Dysplasia.
evidence.snippet: The scalp hair is thin, lightly pigmented, and slow growing.

nodes.description: Absent or severely reduced NF-κB signaling during embryonic development leads to failure of eccrine sweat gland morphogenesis. The resulting hypohidrosis or anhidrosis causes impaired thermoregulation and heat intolerance, which is the most clinically significant feature of HED.

evidence.reference_title: Gene defect in ectodermal dysplasia implicates a death domain adapter in development.
evidence.snippet: This disorder is characterized by sparse hair, a lack of sweat glands and malformation of teeth.

nodes.description: NF-κB signaling through the EDA pathway is required for tooth bud initiation and morphogenesis. Loss of EDARADD leads to oligodontia or anodontia, with conical or peg-shaped teeth when present.

evidence.reference_title: Hypohidrotic Ectodermal Dysplasia.
evidence.snippet: Only a few abnormally formed teeth erupt, at a later-than-average age.

nodes.description: Ocular surface dryness due to defective meibomian and lacrimal gland development, requiring lubrication eye drops. Annual ophthalmologic assessment is recommended.

evidence.reference_title: Hypohidrotic Ectodermal Dysplasia.
evidence.snippet: Lubrication eye drops.

nodes.description: Abnormal nail development, reflecting the ectodermal origin of nail structures and their dependence on EDA pathway signaling.

evidence.reference_title: Novel homozygous frameshift insertion variant in the last exon of the EDARADD causing hypohidrotic ectodermal dysplasia in two siblings: case report and review of the literature.
evidence.snippet: predominantly affects the hair, nails, eccrine glands, and teeth

nodes.description: Periorbital wrinkling, a characteristic facial feature of HED contributing to the distinctive facial gestalt.

evidence.reference_title: Novel homozygous frameshift insertion variant in the last exon of the EDARADD causing hypohidrotic ectodermal dysplasia in two siblings: case report and review of the literature.
evidence.snippet: mildly prominent forehead, and periorbital wrinkles

nodes.description: Mildly prominent forehead, part of the characteristic facial dysmorphism of HED.

evidence.reference_title: Novel homozygous frameshift insertion variant in the last exon of the EDARADD causing hypohidrotic ectodermal dysplasia in two siblings: case report and review of the literature.
evidence.snippet: mildly prominent forehead, and periorbital wrinkles

nodes.description: Recurrent upper and lower respiratory tract infections due to defective airway mucosal gland development and impaired mucociliary clearance.

evidence.reference_title: Hypohidrotic Ectodermal Dysplasia.
evidence.snippet: Management of recurrent respiratory infections and asthma per primary care provider with referral to allergist and/or pulmonologist as needed.

nodes.description: Reduced density of eyebrow hair due to impaired hair follicle development in ectodermal appendages.

evidence.reference_title: Novel homozygous frameshift insertion variant in the last exon of the EDARADD causing hypohidrotic ectodermal dysplasia in two siblings: case report and review of the literature.
evidence.snippet: sparse eyebrows and eyelashes

nodes.description: Reduced density of eyelashes due to impaired hair follicle development.

evidence.reference_title: Novel homozygous frameshift insertion variant in the last exon of the EDARADD causing hypohidrotic ectodermal dysplasia in two siblings: case report and review of the literature.
evidence.snippet: sparse eyebrows and eyelashes

nodes.description: Sparse, fine, lightly pigmented, and slow-growing scalp hair with reduced body hair.

evidence.reference_title: Hypohidrotic Ectodermal Dysplasia.
evidence.snippet: The scalp hair is thin, lightly pigmented, and slow growing.

evidence.reference_title: Gene Mutations of the Three Ectodysplasin Pathway Key Players (EDA, EDAR, and EDARADD) Account for More than 60% of Egyptian Ectodermal Dysplasia: A Report of Seven Novel Mutations.
evidence.snippet: The most common ED phenotype is hypohidrotic/anhidrotic ectodermal dysplasia (HED) featuring hypotrichosis, hypohidrosis/anhidrosis, and hypodontia.

nodes.description: Recessive homozygous frameshift insertion (c.322_323insCGGGC) in the last exon of EDARADD, introducing a premature stop codon. Identified in two consanguineous siblings with classical HED phenotype.

Recessive missense variant in the death domain of EDARADD. The first EDARADD mutation identified in a human HED family. Retains partial ability to activate NF-κB, consistent with recessive inheritance.
Recessive in-frame deletion of two residues in the death domain. Identified in a consanguineous family. Impairs the EDAR-EDARADD interaction and severely inhibits NF-κB activity.
